description: Lock the corrected aviation/aerodynamics chronology: supersonic-flow science predates powered aircraft and jet engines, while jet aircraft remain downstream of jet engines and materials.

checks.reason: Mach's shock-wave photography anchors the broad scientific supersonic-aerodynamics node before aircraft applications.
Supersonic-flow science began before powered aircraft and should not inherit aviation application prerequisites.
Jet engines are later applications, not prerequisites, of supersonic-flow theory and experiments.
The lightweight-alloys node is anchored to discovered duralumin, not a post-1945 placeholder.
The jet-engine node is anchored to Whittle's first running turbojet bench engine.
The jet-aircraft node is anchored to the first Heinkel He 178 jet flight.
Supersonic aerodynamics should remain upstream context for high-speed jet-aircraft development.
